commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: In the law of the true God it is written, “He that sacrifices to gods shall be put to death, save only to the Lord.” The dreadful sanction of this command makes it clear that God wanted no sacrifices offered to such gods, good or bad.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Death. Hebrew, "shall be anathema "(érom) which denotes utter destruction both of the person and of his goods. (Jonathan) (1 Kings xv. 3.)
